summary.intent: Fix three automated code review comments from Greptile on PR #527 in the moltis repo, then resolve the comments.
summary.outcome: All three issues were fixed (dead variable removed, misleading comment corrected, CSS class selectors replaced with semantic locators), linted, committed, and pushed.

repo: CLAUDE.md mandates using getByTitle()/getByRole() instead of CSS class selectors in E2E tests.
The session-header component renders a span with title='Click to rename' when canRename is true, which persists after a rename completes and the input is replaced — making it a stable locator.

code.finding: isChannel variable was defined but never referenced; safe to remove entirely.
E2E test had a misleading comment ('set a channel binding') when the action was just setting a label, and used CSS class selectors instead of semantic locators.

workflow: Greptile is configured as an automated reviewer on PRs and flags dead code, misleading comments, and non-semantic test locators.
Lint and a quick compile check were run before committing review fixes to catch any regressions.

friction: Before removing isChannel, a grep was needed to confirm it was truly unused — the variable name was generic enough to warrant verification.
